name: Urban Upbound
description_short: Non-profit organization providing free financial counseling, credit union services, and comprehensive economic support to low-income New York City residents in public housing.
description_long: Urban Upbound was founded in 2004 (originally as East River Development Alliance) by Debra-Ellen Glickstein and Bishop Mitchell Taylor to address poverty cycles in New York City public housing and low-income neighborhoods. The organization has grown to serve over 38,500 New Yorkers in the past three years through an intensive case management model that provides integrated services addressing resident-identified needs. Their mission centers on breaking cycles of poverty through seven comprehensive programs designed to build economic self-sufficiency. The organization operates the Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union, which serves as a critical anchor providing financial products and asset-building opportunities unavailable through mainstream banks. Urban Upbound distinguishes itself through its long-term engagement model where clients receive coordinated access across all program areas rather than siloed services. Their client base consists primarily of NYCHA (New York City Housing Authority) residents and other low-income New Yorkers, with particular focus on youth and families. The organization returned $25.77 million in tax refunds to the community over three years and achieved a 100% college acceptance rate among college access participants. Urban Upbound is a legitimate non-profit serving a genuinely underserved population: nearly 98,000 NYCHA residents lack access to banking or credit union services, and public housing residents earn only 35% of the city's average household income. However, as a localized non-profit, services are limited to New York City residents, and capacity constraints mean waitlists may exist for some programs. The organization's credit union membership and financial services require local presence or remote eligibility verification.
services: Free tax preparation and income support services
Financial counseling and affordable housing assistance
Employment services including job readiness training and placement
College access programs and youth development
Vocational training in security, healthcare, administration, culinary arts, and customer service
NYC Department of Buildings 10-hour Site Safety Training (SST)
One-on-one career counseling and job retention support
Worker cooperative development and business innovation programs
Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union membership and financial products
Computer lab access for job searching and virtual training
pros: Operates Urban Upbound Federal Credit Union offering credit-building and asset products specifically designed for underserved residents excluded from mainstream banking
Provides free tax preparation and income support services, returning $25.77M to the community in three years
Achieved 100% college acceptance rate for college access program participants
Offers free financial counseling and credit counseling integrated with employment and housing services
Provides no-cost job training including OSHA 30 construction certification and Site Safety Training
Uses intensive case management approach ensuring clients access full array of integrated services over extended periods
Serves 38,500+ New Yorkers in three years with comprehensive, evidence-based programming
cons: Services limited exclusively to New York City residents, primarily NYCHA public housing residents and low-income neighborhoods—not available nationally or in other states
As a non-profit with capacity constraints, program availability and waitlists are not detailed; may have enrollment limitations
No information provided about credit union membership requirements, eligibility criteria, or specific loan/savings products offered
best_for: NYCHA public housing residents in New York City seeking free financial counseling and credit building
Low-income New Yorkers excluded from traditional banking needing employment training, tax prep, and financial services
Young adults in NYC public housing pursuing college access with integrated support services
Queens residents needing affordable credit union membership and long-term financial stability building
diagnosis: Urban Upbound is best for low-income New York City residents, particularly NYCHA public housing residents, seeking free integrated financial counseling, credit building through their federal credit union, and wraparound support for employment and housing stability. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to New York City only, making it inaccessible to consumers outside the five boroughs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Non-profit providing free financial counseling, credit union services, and economic support to low-income NYC residents in public housing.
